sections.paragraph:
κέντρον, ου, τό (κεντέω ‘prick, spur on’; Hom. et al.; BGU 544, 12; LXX; PsSol 16:4; JosAs 16:13; Philo; Jos., Bell. 2, 385, Ant. 7, 169; Ath. 13:1) gener. ‘sharp point’.

① the sting of an animal (Aristot. et al.; Aelian, NA 16, 27 σκορπίου) Rv 9:10 (s. Ctesias: 688 Fgm. 45 p. 490, 1 Jac. [Indica 7], a strange beast of India τὸ πρόσωπον ἐοικὸς ἀνθρώπῳ … ὥσπερ λέων … horrible teeth … σκορπίος … τὸ κέντρον in its tail, whose sting is deadly). In imagery (Aesop, Fab. 276 P. [also H-H. 273 app.]=Babr. no. 185 Cr. κ. τῆς λύπης) of death 1 Cor 15:55f after Hos 13:14 (s. ESellin, RSeeberg Festschr. I 1929, 307–14, cp. Straub 35; the imagery is transcultural: a friend of a teacher named Theodoros records in an epitaph that he felt a κέντρον ἄπαυστον, ‘unceasing sting’ because of his death [Kaibel 534, 8=Peek, GVI 1479, 8, s. New Docs 4, 157 no. 64]).

② a pointed stick that serves the same purpose as a whip, a goad (Hom. et al.; Pr 26:3), in a proverbial expr. (Pind., P. 2, 94 [173] [s. Ael. Aristid. 45 p. 70 D.]; Aeschyl., Ag. 1624, Prom. 323; Eur., Bacch. 795 [WNestle, Anklänge an Eur. in AG: Philol. 59, 1900, 46–57]; Fgm. Iamb. Adesp. 13 in AnthLG [D-B.] III 75: ἵππος ὄνῳ· ‘πρὸς κέντρα μὴ λακτιζέτω’ ‘a horse to an ass: “No kicking against the goads!”’ [The cj. λάκτιζέ πω proposed by Crusius—s. JEdmonds, ed., Greek Elegy and Iambus II, ’31, repr. ’79, p. 310 no. 64—is unnecessary]; ins fr. Asia Minor [JHS 8, 1887, 261]: λακτίζεις πρὸς κέντρα; AOtto, D. Sprichwörter d. Römer 1890, 331f) πρὸς κέντρα λακτίζειν kick against the goads of a balking animal, fig. of one who resists a divine call Ac 9:4 v.l.; 26:14 (on the pl. cp. Eur., loc. cit., the iambic fragment, the ins, and PGM 4, 2911 κέντροισι βιαίοις of the stings of passion; Herm. Wr. p. 482, 26 Sc.; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 46 πάθους κέντροις).—FSmend, Αγγελος I 1925, 34–45, esp. 41ff, but s. WKümmel, Rö 7 u. die Bekehrung des Paulus 1929, 155–57; HWindisch, ZNW 31, ’32, 10–14; further lit. in Haenchen ad loc.—B. 864. DELG s.v. κεντέω. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Ctesias Ctesias , V–IVb.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Straub Straub = WS., Die Bildersprache des Apostels Paulus—List 6

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

GVI GVI = Griechische Vers-inschriften I: Die Grabepigramma—List 3

expr. expr. = expression

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

AG AG = Apostelgeschichte (in German titles relating to Acts)

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

AnthLG AnthLG = Anthologia Lyrica Graeca—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6
  2. cj. = conjecture(d by)
  3. ed. = edited (by), edition

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

JHS JHS = Journal of Hellenic Studies—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. pl. = plural

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 539
